_comment: Battle AI phase 3 (#586): the general's reserve pool and its reserve-commit morale threshold. Freshly authored for this phase -- not copied from any other PR's scripted-input file (see .claude/memories/sparta-demos.md's 'author each demo scenario fresh' entry). Forces team 1 onto the 'cautious' doctrine (data/doctrines/cautious.json: reserve_fraction 0.4, reserve_commit_morale_threshold 30.0, pursue_routers false) via the input script's own 'doctrine' field. Team 1 has 5 units, staged in uid order so three understrength, low-morale Infantry (uid 1-3, 30 soldiers, morale 32 -- just above the commit threshold) spawn before two full-strength units (uid 4-5, a Cavalry and an Infantry) -- General._reserve_uids holds back the highest-uid fraction (2 of 5), so uid 4-5 are the ones held in reserve, HOLD_LINE-pinned at their own spawn point (General.reserve_directives) far from the front (beyond UnitLeader.RELIEF_CALL_RANGE, so the ordinary inter-unit relief call -- ALSO a real mechanic, but a different one -- can't reach them and confound the demo; see this file's own PR discussion). Verified by state dump (tools/demo/dump-state.sh): uid 1-3 alone meet the single full-strength enemy Infantry and take losses; by tick 240 their weighted-average morale has fallen to roughly 20 (one of the three, uid 3, has already routed), crossing the 30 threshold, and General.decide_army folds uid 4-5 into the active line that same tick -- their current_order flips from null (held) to ATTACK within the next few ticks, visible on screen as the two reserves breaking their stationary hold and marching in; both are in melee by tick 600-700. Static wide camera (no pan) keeps the whole field -- both the front clash and the waiting reserve pair -- in view throughout, so the hold-then-commit transition reads clearly without needing a mid-clip move.
